the sim opens on Solaris, which the field rarely builds (1.5% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Bayle (it never builds Overlord, Cursed Scroll) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime, extended fights, physical survival and magical survival: first spike lands around minute 12 (Solaris), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +13.2% on your burst combo at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 6,277 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 25 — about 749 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +52% more 10s rotation damage: try Solaris over Earthshaker (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 646 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +32% more one-combo burst: try Solaris over Overlord (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 31% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Crunch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 17% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Frenzied Fighter” ⇒ sustain; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field offlane 49.9% over 256,859 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Your kill threat peaks early-to-mid: cash it in before their later items level the exchange. Feng Mao deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 22% of its bite; you give up 25% of your damage.
